introText: Learn about Medicaid eligibility requirements in Alabama, including income limits, asset requirements, and how to apply for coverage in 2026.

applicationMethods.description: Apply online through Alabama's Medicaid portal
Call your local Department of Social Services
Visit your local county office

programs.name: Aged, Blind, and Disabled
programs.description: Coverage for seniors 65+ and people with disabilities
programs.eligibility: Income below SSI limits

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services
programs.description: Waiver program for in-home care as an alternative to nursing facilities
programs.eligibility: Nursing home level of care needed

faqs.question: What are the income limits for Medicaid in Alabama?
faqs.answer: In Alabama, the income limit for Medicaid eligibility is generally 138% of the Federal Poverty Level for most adults. Seniors and individuals with disabilities may have different limits. Contact your local office for exact figures.

faqs.question: How do I apply for Medicaid in Alabama?
faqs.answer: You can apply online, by phone, by mail, or in person at your local county office. Processing typically takes 30-45 days.

programs.name: Aged, Blind, and Disabled
programs.description: Coverage for seniors 65+ in Topeka with disabilities
programs.eligibility: Income below SSI limits

faqs.question: What are the Medicaid income limits in Topeka, KS?

faqs.question: Where can I apply for Medicaid in Topeka?

faqs.question: Does Kansas Medicaid cover home care in Topeka?
